prompt: What does Alleato consider best practice for handling owner-driven scope changes?

score.finalText:
I don’t have a documented Alleato best-practice article on that yet.

What I checked:
- **Company knowledge base:** no best-practice entry for owner-driven scope changes
- **App help:** no workflow article for managing change events/scope changes; the returned help results were unrelated

So I’m not going to pretend there’s an official Alleato standard if it isn’t in the system.

What I can tell you instead is the practical standard this should follow in a construction environment:

1. **Separate the request from approval**
   - Log the owner request immediately as a potential change.
   - Do **not** let field execution treat it as approved scope until pricing, schedule impact, and authorization path are clear.

2. **Write the scope change down in plain language**
   - What changed
   - Why it changed
   - Who requested it
   - What original scope it affects
   - What decision date is needed to avoid delay

3. **Price and time it together**
   - Best practice is never to price scope without schedule impact.
   - Every owner-driven change should carry:
     - cost impact
     - schedule impact
     - procurement impact
     - design/coordination impact

4. **Push it through a formal change event / change order workflow**
   - Track status visibly: identified, pricing in progress, submitted, pending approval, approved, executed.
   - Keep backup attached: quotes, sketches, emails, assumptions, exclusions.

5. **Document assumptions and exclusions early**
   - This is where margin gets protected.
   - If the owner wants speed before documents are complete, qualify the price clearly.

6. **Assign one owner internally**
   - One PM or project lead owns the ball.
   - If operations, estimating, and accounting all think someone else has it, it will sit and become dispute fuel.

7. **Do not let unpriced work pile up**
   - The real risk is not the single change. It’s volume.
   - Once owner-driven changes start moving faster than pricing/approval, you get hidden exposure and credibility problems.

8. **Reconcile field execution against paper weekly**
   - Ask: what are we building that is not yet fully approved?
   - That weekly discipline is what keeps “helpful accommodation” from turning into unrecoverable cost.

My point of view:  
**Best practice is disciplined separation of requested scope, approved scope, and performed scope.** That’s what protects margin, schedule credibility, and owner trust.

If you want, I can do one of two useful next steps:
- draft a **formal Alleato best-practice entry** for the knowledge base on owner-driven scope changes, or
- turn this into a **one-page SOP/checklist** your PMs can use on live jobs.

results.title: Manage User Access
results.description: Manage users, roles, project access, and sensitive modules.

actionCapabilities.label: Assign access
actionCapabilities.description: Assign company or project roles to a user after reviewing access needs.
actionCapabilities.unavailableReason: The assistant can explain access decisions, but access updates require a future preview-and-confirm tool.

actionCapabilities.label: Update role
actionCapabilities.description: Change a reusable company or project role.
actionCapabilities.unavailableReason: Template changes can affect many users, so this remains guidance-only until a dedicated review workflow exists.

results.excerpt: Manage User Access User Management controls which tools a person can see and what they can do inside those tools. Use it carefully because access changes can expose project, financial, document, and directory information. Company-Level Access 1. Open User Management from the company tools area. 2. results.title: Create or Invite a User
results.description: Add an app user from User Management and explain how the invited person finishes login setup.

actionCapabilities.label: Invite user
actionCapabilities.description: Prepare and send an invitation for a new Alleato OS user from User Management.
actionCapabilities.unavailableReason: The assistant can explain the invitation flow, but the invite-user write tool is not implemented yet.

results.excerpt: Create or Invite a User New users are added from User Management. In the current workflow, an administrator creates the user record, chooses the user's access, and sends an invitation email. The invited person accepts the invitation and creates their own password. The user's email address is their username. There is no separate username to give them. Steps 1. Open User Management. 2. Stay on the **App Users** tab. 3.

results.title: Ask the AI Assistant for Help
results.description: Use Ask Alleato when you need help finding information or understanding how to use the platform.
results.excerpt: Ask the AI Assistant for Help Use Ask Alleato when you need help finding information, understanding a workflow, or getting oriented inside the platform. The assistant can answer questions from available project data, help documentation, and approved knowledge sources. When an answer depends on data that is not indexed or a workflow that is not documented yet, it should say so plainly instead of guessing. Good Questio

results.title: Navigate Projects
results.description: Switch between projects, open the project home page, and find project tools.
results.excerpt: Navigate Projects Projects are the main workspace in Alleato OS. Most project tools use the selected project context, so confirm the right project before reviewing or changing project information. Switch Projects 1. Use **Select Project** in the header. 2. Search for the project by name or job number. 3. Select the project from the list. 4. Use View all projects if you need to browse the full project list. After you

results.title: Update Your Profile
results.description: Review your profile details and update your profile image.

actionCapabilities.label: Update profile
actionCapabilities.description: Update profile details or profile image for the signed-in user or an admin-managed user record.
actionCapabilities.unavailableReason: The assistant can guide profile updates, but profile writes are not exposed as an assistant action yet.

results.excerpt: Update Your Profile Your profile shows the account details Alleato OS uses to identify you across the platform. Steps 1. Open the user menu in the top-right corner. 2. Select Profile. 3. Review your name, email, title, company, phone, location, timezone, region, and role. 4. To update your profile image, use the profile image upload control. memories.content: The user prefers detailed, specific updates on project status, risks, and next steps for Vermillion Rise Warehouse.
User prefers high-leverage, action-oriented tasks prioritized daily for Vermillion Rise Warehouse project.
User prefers to have tasks and RFIs associated with specific project names, due dates, and assignees documented clearly.
